title: valeriana excelsa subsp. versifolia
intro: Valeriana excelsa subsp. versifolia, also known as the Himalayan valerian, is a plant species that belongs to the valerian family (Valerianaceae) and is native to the Himalayas. The medicinal properties of Valeriana excelsa subsp. versifolia lie in its roots, which contain valerenic acid and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. The health benefits of this plant include reducing stress and anxiety levels, promoting relaxation and sleep, and acting as a natural sedative, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Valeriana excelsa subsp. versifolia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
